draft.introduction: Matthew’s infancy narrative presents the virgin birth as fulfillment of Isaiah 7:14. Joseph’s decision to take Mary as his wife despite her pregnancy is guided by an angel in a dream.

translation.text: Now the birth of Jesus Christ took place in this way. When his mother Mary had been betrothed to Joseph, before they came together she was found to be with child from the Holy Spirit. And her husband Joseph, being a just man and unwilling to put her to shame, resolved to divorce her quietly. But an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream.
translation.original_text: τοῦ δὲ Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ ἡ γένεσις οὕτως ἦν

translation.text: All this took place to fulfill what the Lord had spoken by the prophet: “Behold, the virgin shall conceive and bear a son, and they shall call his name Immanuel” (which means, God with us). And he called his name Jesus.

translator_notes.note: Matthew quotes Isaiah 7:14 from the LXX, using παρθένος (“virgin”). The name Immanuel (“God with us”) forms an inclusio with the Gospel’s final verse: “I am with you always, to the end of the age” (28:20).
